questions.question_text: Why does finance say churn is 4.1% but product says it's 6.8%?
questions.answer_summary: Finance measures logo churn on billing date; product measures it on last-login date. Customers who stop using the product but haven't cancelled yet create a 2.7-point gap that delays churn recognition by 45 days on average.

questions.question_text: Is our ARR number board-ready or are we counting multi-year deals wrong?
questions.answer_summary: Multi-year contracts with step-up pricing are annualized at the full contract value instead of current-year rate. This inflates ARR by $1.9M and misrepresents growth trajectory to investors.

questions.question_text: Are our cohort retention curves actually improving or is it a data artifact?
questions.answer_summary: The 2024 cohorts look 12% better because the analytics team changed the activation definition mid-year. Under the original definition, retention is flat quarter over quarter.

questions.question_text: How much of our expansion revenue is real upsell vs. pricing corrections?
questions.answer_summary: 28% of what's reported as expansion revenue is actually mid-cycle pricing adjustments and overage charges. True upsell from new seats or tier upgrades is $840K lower than the board deck shows.

questions.question_text: Can we trust the pipeline coverage ratio we're using for forecasting?
questions.answer_summary: Pipeline includes 34 opportunities that haven't had activity in 90+ days and 12 duplicates from CRM migration. Cleaning these drops coverage from 3.8x to 2.6x, below the 3x threshold for the quarter.
